# Mohammad Raouf Abedini - Cybersecurity & Software Engineering Portfolio

## Identity
Name: Mohammad Raouf Abedini (Raouf)
Role: Cybersecurity Specialist, Software Engineer
Location: Castle Hill, NSW, Australia
Focus: Privacy-first security tools, full-stack engineering, AI-native platforms, eBPF runtime security.

## Core Competencies (Keywords)
- Offensive Security: Penetration Testing, Red Teaming, Threat Modelling, LLM Red Teaming.
- Defensive Security: eBPF Runtime Security, Network Forensics, Privacy Engineering, Zero-Trust Architecture.
- Engineering: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Kotlin Multiplatform, Python, Litestar, Supabase, Docker, Terraform.

## Featured Projects (The "Truth" for Retrieval)
1. Mehr Guard: Privacy-first mobile security app for Android/iOS. Offline QR & URL threat scanning with ensemble ML + 25 heuristics. Kotlin Multiplatform, 5 platform targets.
2. Syllabus-Sync: AI-native Campus OS for Australian universities. LLM OCR pipeline transforms PDF syllabi into structured data. Next.js 16, Supabase, WebAuthn passkeys, 503 tests across 92 files.
3. GitSwitch: AI-powered Git client for managing multiple identities and generating semantic commits. Electron, React 19, Gemini AI.
4. Nexus Archive: Cyberpunk-styled personal media vault. React 19 frontend, Litestar API, Supabase PostgreSQL. HttpOnly cookie auth, encrypted takeaways, AI recommendations.
5. eBPF Cloud Runtime Security Monitor (ECRSM): Educational read-only runtime visibility stack using kernel eBPF tracepoints, Go agent, and React dashboard.

## Philosophy
Raouf operates at the intersection of "Build" (Scalable Architecture) and "Secure" (Defensive Design). He advocates for privacy-by-design, local-first processing, and security embedded from design to deployment.
